candidate_problems.text: What specific architectural additions are sufficient to turn token prediction into an information-selecting process governed by criticism, memory, and explanatory constraint, rather than merely a more elaborate recombination system?

How can one distinguish, in operational terms, a system whose internal informational possibilities have genuinely changed from one that only appears more capable because external human critics are supplying the decisive selection?

Does recursive search-and-critique with memory actually escape 'inductive closure,' or can it be shown to remain confined to transformations of already-implicit informational patterns in the training ecology?
What would count as a concrete case of machine-generated explanation that solves a problem better than before without relying on human judgment for the critical step?
